objects.name: Analytic 1812
objects.description: A defender correlates an application being granted accessibility service control with subsequent consumption of high-volume accessibility events, interaction with sensitive UI elements or text-entry fields, optional overlay/window presentation over other applications, and near-term local buffering or outbound network transmission, indicating abuse of accessibility features for input capture, credential theft, or automated interaction.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application remains backgrounded while accessibility service continues to receive events or perform actions across other foreground apps

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Accessibility framework usage patterns such as event subscription, performAction invocation, node traversal, text change observation, or overlay/window presentation correlated to app identity

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Allow-list of sanctioned accessibility-enabled apps in the environment, such as screen readers or approved assistive tools.
Threshold for event volume or sustained event consumption indicating broad UI monitoring rather than limited assistive use.
Determines whether detection should require correlation to text-entry fields, login screens, or password/credential UI contexts.
Time window correlating accessibility activity with overlay/window presentation over other apps.
Time window linking accessibility event capture or text change activity to outbound network communication.
Tuning for whether background accessibility service activity is expected for approved assistive tools.
Minimum outbound byte volume or burst count considered suspicious after accessibility event capture.
